text: The surgeon encounters filmy adhesions in the gallbladder area, with Rouvier's sulcus visible behind the adhesions.
The surgeon identifies and dissects the cystic artery and cystic duct, achieving the critical view of safety. The cystic artery is visible as it is being carefully dissected.

The surgeon alternates between immunofluorescence imaging and standard view, performing a straightforward dissection to achieve the critical view of safety. The cystic duct is clearly visible leading directly into the gallbladder, with the liver seen in the background.
The surgeon places two clips on the stay side and one clip on the specimen side of the cystic duct during the robotic cholecystectomy procedure.
The surgeon observes the cystic plate, noting its average width and the thinning at the top. The gallbladder is visible, and care is taken to avoid inadvertent cholecystotomies.
